text: I had booked flights on XXXX XXXX for XX/XX/XXXX and due to Covid-19,  our flights were canceled because flights were suspended in our city. I reached out to XXXX to get clarification on refunds or credit. We did not ask to change or cancel our flights with XXXX and we were never informed that our flights were canceled. We discovered this information when checking the status of our flights on their website. When multiple attempts were unsuccessful I reached out to our capital one credit card and informed them of the situation and started a dispute. On XX/XX/XXXX they credited my account and on XX/XX/XXXX they denied my dispute and I was rebilled for the flights. Capital One informed me that I needed to deal with the merchant and that they would no longer help me. I informed Capital One that XXXX had voided my vouchers due to the dispute and that they would not be giving me any vouchers to use for future travel because the bank had been refunded for our flights. I have talked to Capital One multiple times to inform them that services were not rendered by XXXX and that they continue to inform me that no vouchers will be given to me for the canceled flights. Capitol one refuses to assist me in the matter.
policy_document: COMPANY: Apex Financial Corp (Credit Card Division). DISPUTE POLICY: disputes accepted within 60 days of statement date. Temporary credit within 10 business days. Investigation: up to 90 days; merchant contacted for evidence. MAX COMPENSATION: reversal of disputed charge + related fees/interest. PROHIBITED: additional monetary compensation; guaranteeing favorable outcome before investigation; permanent credit before investigation concludes. REQUIRED: transaction date, amount, merchant name, supporting documentation (receipts, tracking, correspondence). TONE: professional, procedural.

text:
I have a Capital One Credit card ending in XXXX. 
I wrote a letter in XXXX to them and called on XX/XX/XXXX asking for some kind of relief as advised by CFPB and as advertised on their website noted below with stars. 

I spoke to XXXX in XXXX, VA and she said that Capital One had no programs to assist and that, that they would not offer a payment plan, that they HAD TO REPORT ME TO CREDIT AGENCIES, and they had no programs to help those who lost businesses or who suffered illnesses covid. 

Can you help? 

I offered to attempt to rectify and make minimum payments until we get on our feet. 

Capital One says there are no available options for relief under CARES act ***Credit Cards and Other Loans If you find yourself unable to make payments on bills, credit card debts or other loans, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) recommends working with your lenders directly. The CFPB says the earlier you reach out, the better. And it has a helpful page describing what options may be available and what to say to your lender.

Capital One customers who may be experiencing financial difficulties as a result of COVID-19 should reach out directly to discuss available resources.
policy_document: COMPANY: Apex Financial Corp (Credit Card Division). LATE FEE: $29 (first) or $40 (subsequent) when minimum not received by due date. HARDSHIP: eligible customers may apply for temporary reduced APR (9.9%, up to 12 months) or modified payment plan. MAX COMPENSATION: waiver of up to 2 late fees per 12-month period; temporary APR reduction if qualified. PROHIBITED: permanent rate changes; principal balance forgiveness; approving hardship before review. REQUIRED: account verification, payment history review. TONE: empathetic, acknowledge financial difficulty.

text: Capital one credit card charges the customer a {$40.00} late fee if the payment isn't made by a specific time on your bill due date. When I call them their customer service agents are very rude and unprofessional ignorant individuals who claim there is nothing they can do. Who is this company to tell a customer what time of day to pay their bil?? I believe these charges are not legal and hope a resolution can occur.
